practices.name: running
practices.description: Half-marathon training — runs, paces, long runs, niggles.

obs.body: Rent went out, account looks grim till payday. Need to chase Dev about his half of the broadband again.
Late shift done. Down a HCA most of the evening, picked up the obs run for bay 4 as well. Fine, just long.
Easy 5k, legs felt heavy the first km then opened up. 5:48 pace, didn't push.
Day shift. Steady, good team on. Got a proper lunch in for once, sat down a full half hour.
Ordered the Saucony Endorphins everyone bangs on about. Reviews are unhinged. Apparently they're 'cheating'.
Tempo, 6k with 4 at threshold. Felt strong, hit 4:42 for the middle bit. Day off tomorrow.
Long run done early, 14k along the canal. Comfortable, negative split even. Best I've felt in weeks.
New shoes arrived. Did a lap of the block in them in the kitchen basically. Bouncy. Logging this so I remember I caved.
Day shift, mellow. Tried the shoes on the walk to the bus, ridiculous but I get the hype.
4k recovery jog in the new ones. They do feel fast? Or I'm imagining it. 5:30 without trying.
First night of the block. Resus busy from 9 onwards, never stopped. Bank nurse cancelled so we ran short again.
Night two. Two off sick, no replacements. Didn't sit down till 5am and that was to chart. Wrecked.
Slept till 3. Supposed to do an easy 6, couldn't make myself. Just lay there.
Night three. Mad one, full department, corridor patients. No break again, ate a cereal bar standing at the desk.
Last night done. Four in a row. Body feels like it belongs to someone else. Sleep is the only plan.
Throat's a bit scratchy. Something going round maybe, or just run-down from the mileage. Dunno.
Meant to do the long one today, 16k on the plan. Slept till 1pm. Got out for 6 and bonked hard at 4. Crawled home.
Off. Did absolutely nothing. Watched four episodes of that submarine boxset Dev's obsessed with. Worth it.
Easy 5k, throat better. Legs came back a bit. 5:40, fine.
Day shift. Calm-ish. Full staff for once, even had a spare pair of hands. Felt like a holiday.
Days again. Decent. Got my break, sat in the garden bit out back. Sun out.
Tempo session, 8k total, 5 at threshold. Flew. 4:38 average for the block.
Bought a tub of that chocolate protein. Going to have one after every run, see if recovery's better.
Long run, 15k. Strong the whole way, even threw in some surges at the end. Protein shake after. Felt great.
Car park guy at the hospital booked me AGAIN for the staff bay. I have a permit. Furious. Appealing it.
Day shift. Standard. Permit dispute rumbling on, emailed the trust parking office, no reply obviously.
Easy 6k. Had the shake after again. Honestly can't tell if it does anything but the runs are good lately.
Mrs H in bay 2 today. Long story, family at the bedside, the lot. Held it together on shift, cried in the car after. Some shifts stay with you.
Couldn't face a run yesterday after that. Did 8k today instead, just to move. Settled my head a bit.
Days. Quiet morning, busy after lunch but staffing held. Switched the protein to vanilla, tastes like wallpaper paste.
Long run a day early, 16k, nailed it. 5:20 pace and could've gone further. This is the level I want to hold.
Dev's leaving-do for his old job tonight. Going to be a write-off. Had four pints before I remembered I run.
Tried a run hungover from Dev's thing. Death. Stopped at 3k and walked. Never again (this week).
Back on nights. Block of four starting. Short one nurse from the off, agency didn't show. Heaving in majors.
Night two. Brutal. Two unwell patients went off at once, all hands, no break, no chance of one. Charting at handover I could barely see straight.
Cold sore coming up on my lip. Run-down I guess. Could be the training load, could be I've not eaten properly in days.
Night three. Down two HCAs this time. Did obs, meds and basically the care assistant job all night. Didn't sit.
Fourth night done. Place was a warzone, four short across the team by 3am when someone went home sick. Empty.
Phone died completely, won't charge. Great timing, payday's not till Friday. Using Dev's old one for now.
Long run on the plan was 18k. Got 7. Legs hollow, no power at all, packed it in by the park. Annoyed.
Calf's a bit niggly on the left. Could be the speedwork last week, could be I've upped the mileage too quick. Hard to say.
Off. Sorted the phone, just needed a new cable in the end, four quid. Felt like a genius. Boxset finale tonight.
Gentle 5k to test the calf. Held up. Took it easy, 5:55, no point risking it.
Day shift, back to days thank god. Fully staffed even. Coasted, in a good way. Break and everything.
Days. Decent crew on, manageable. Parking appeal actually got upheld, fine cancelled. Small victories.
Tempo, 7k with 5 at threshold. Calf totally fine. 4:44 for the block, smooth. Back in business.
Long run, 17k. Lovely. Steady 5:18, finished fresh, big coffee after.
Dev moved his bike into the hallway and now nobody can get past. Flat politics. Sent a passive-aggressive text.
Day shift. Busy but staffed properly, that's the difference. Got everything done and still left on time.
Easy 6k. Legs springy. 5:25 felt like nothing. Good patch right now.
Heatwave hit. Tried a long run at 3pm like an idiot, 28 degrees, abandoned at 9k, felt sick. That was the heat, pure and simple.
Day shift, sweltering on the ward, AC half working. Still, staffing fine, just hot. Drank about six litres.
Redid the long run early before the heat, 18k at 5am. 5:15 pace, beautiful and empty out there.
Days. Steady. New starter shadowing me, actually nice to have an extra body. Showed her the resus trolley.
Intervals, 6x800. Hit them all under target. 3:08 per rep. Flying. Best block of training I've had.
Nights again, three this time. First one and we're short already, one called in sick at 8pm, no cover.
Night two. Relentless. Two ambulances queued outside half the shift. No break, ate nothing till the drive home.
Last night. Down a nurse and a HCA. Ran on fumes, did the whole obs round myself twice. Cannot describe the tiredness.
Long run was meant to be the big 19k taper-peak today. Got out, managed 8, legs just wouldn't fire. Walked the last bit home.
Off, slept till noon. Throat scratchy again. Could be the ward lurgy going round, could be I've burned the candle both ends. Tea and toast and nothing.
Day shift, gentle reintroduction to the human race. Staffed, calm, sat down twice. Felt almost normal.
Easy 6k, throat clearing. Legs coming back already. 5:35, no drama.
Days, last of the stretch. Good shift, properly staffed. Race is in two weeks, weirdly calm about it.
Short sharp 5k tempo, 4:40s, felt great. When the legs are fresh they're really fresh. Booked the train for race day.
